atomicity.message: Staged payload is exactly one release hygiene chore: untrack the two committed dist release artifacts while leaving ignored local copies and deferring all changelog, packaging, tag, release, issue, docs, and tracker-state work to later rel beads.

conditions.message: The staged rel-untrack payload is mechanically clean: it only removes the tracked dist archive and checksum from Git, with no source/style surface added.
evidence: Read backpressure/lint-rules.md; it asks whether applicable lint/style rules were considered and distinguishes attestation review from executable burpvalve lint commands.
git diff --cached --name-status showed exactly two staged deletions: dist/burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz and dist/burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz.sha256.
git diff --cached --check exited 0.
test -z "$(git ls-files dist)" passed and printed 'git ls-files dist: empty'.

Raw go test ./... and go run ./cmd/burpvalve ci --feature could not be used as clean live-worktree evidence because both failed to build an unrelated unstaged internal/backpressure/lint.go hunk: 'no new variables on left side of :='. git diff --cached --name-only confirmed only dist files are staged; git diff --name-status -- internal/backpressure/lint.go showed that lint.go change is unstaged.

conditions.message: The staged payload removes only the two committed dist artifacts and introduces no duplicate logic, docs, or parallel implementation path.
evidence: jq /tmp/rel-untrack-dist-brightowl-packets.json showed the feature payload is exactly deleted dist/burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz and deleted dist/burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz.sha256.
git diff --cached --name-status showed exactly D dist/burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz and D dist/burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz.sha256.
git diff --cached --stat showed only those two files: binary archive 2850829 -> 0 bytes and one checksum-line deletion.

conditions.message: The slice removes stale source-tracked release artifacts instead of preserving a misleading local archive success signal.
evidence: br show project-readiness-kit-rel-untrack-dist-gko --json says the bead exists to stop committing release archives to source because GitHub release assets are canonical and old local tarballs can cause false validation.
git ls-files dist produced no output after the staged deletion, so the archive and checksum are no longer tracked in the index.
git check-ignore -v dist/burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz dist/burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz.sha256 reported .gitignore:10:dist/ for both paths, preventing accidental re-add by default.

conditions.message: No governed one-function-one-test surface exists in this staged payload: it deletes tracked release archive files and does not change executable functions or behavior needing direct tests.
evidence: Read backpressure/one-function-one-test.md; it governs new or changed behavior requiring direct test coverage.
git diff --cached --name-status contains only D dist/burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz and D dist/burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz.sha256.
No Go source, tests, scripts, docs, or config behavior files are staged for this rel-untrack payload.

conditions.message: The staged-slice checks pass; the Burpvalve CI probe identifies the expected hash and blocks only because the attestation has not been written yet.
evidence: git diff --cached --check exited 0 for the staged deletion.
In clean staged snapshot /tmp/rel-untrack-stage.aELZXP, go test ./... passed across cmd/burpvalve and all internal packages.

Clean-source ci probe from /tmp/rel-untrack-stage.aELZXP against --root /home/ubuntu/Projects/burpvalve reported staged_payload_hash sha256:f422bcd7ad33a7e24959cd07771f17c79009944a0b18e899f730150d17662e63 and blocked only on missing backpressure/attestations/f422bcd7ad33a7e24959cd07771f17c79009944a0b18e899f730150d17662e63.json.

conditions.message: The release-artifact untracking claim is backed by direct index, ignore-rule, local-file, test, and CI-hash evidence.
evidence: git ls-files dist and git ls-files --stage dist both produced no output after staging, proving no tracked dist entries remain in the index.

Local file probes showed dist/burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz and dist/burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz.sha256 still exist as ignored working-tree files, so the operation is untracking rather than local deletion.
Clean-source ci probe recorded the same f422bcd7... staged payload hash as the packet.

conditions.message: The staged source payload is exactly the bead's untrack-dist scope and does not include release tagging, docs, issue closure, license, visibility, or lint-chain work.
evidence: git diff --cached --name-status showed only the two dist deletions named in the packet and bead scope.

git diff --cached --name-only | rg '^(skill/burpvalve/SKILL.md|README.md|docs/|\.github/|install.sh|cmd/|internal/|\.beads/)' produced no output, confirming no staged license/visibility/docs/code/beads side effects.
git status --short shows unrelated dirty .beads/issues.jsonl and lint files in the working tree, but they are unstaged and not part of this payload.

conditions.message: The governed deletion is limited to source-index removal of the two approved dist artifacts; local ignored copies remain and no broader destructive operation is staged.
evidence: git diff --cached --summary reported delete mode 100644 only for dist/burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz and dist/burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz.sha256.
Local file probes showed both dist files still present in the working tree, matching git rm --cached behavior rather than filesystem removal.
git diff --cached --name-status showed no staged rm/reset/cleanup outside the two approved dist paths.

conditions.message: The release-artifact integrity model is preserved by removing stale tracked archive/checksum files from source while leaving future release assets canonical outside the repo tree.
evidence: br show project-readiness-kit-rel-untrack-dist-gko --json states GitHub release assets are canonical and local packaging later must use DIST_DIR=$(mktemp -d).
git diff --cached --numstat showed the binary archive deletion plus exactly one checksum-line deletion for dist/burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz.sha256.
git check-ignore -v confirmed both dist artifact paths are ignored by .gitignore:10:dist/, reducing risk that regenerated artifacts are silently reintroduced into source.

verifier.transcript_ref: agent-mail:2461; ruling agent-mail:2467; corroborating evidence agent-mail:2464
verifier.evidence_ref: A14c ruling and merged ScarletMarsh/BrightOwl security-boundaries evidence

conditions.message: Security-boundaries is governed and passes under RusticDog ruling A14c: operations on regulated executable distribution artifacts, including deletions, are governed; this payload only removes the tracked archive and checksum from source control, leaves ignored local copies, and introduces no new executable bytes, credentials, permissions, network behavior, upload, workflow, license, visibility, auth, sandbox, or API change.
evidence: Read backpressure/security-boundaries.md; it governs auth, secrets, permissions, sandbox, and external API safety rules.
git diff --cached --summary shows delete mode 100644 for the archive and checksum only; no new binary, script, workflow, installer, or release credential path is staged.
git diff --cached -- dist/burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz.sha256 shows the removed checksum line only: 2ec24951787fc50da47c34a538da9938014e3dc8d5b42776cec092c579e64907  burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz.
git check-ignore -v confirms local remaining archive/checksum files are ignored by .gitignore:10:dist/ and therefore not tracked source artifacts.
No LICENSE, visibility, GitHub workflow, install.sh, source code, or docs paths are staged in this payload.
git diff --cached --name-status showed only deletion of dist/burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz and dist/burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz.sha256.

git diff --cached --name-only grep for skill/burpvalve/SKILL.md, README.md, docs/, .github/, install.sh, cmd/, internal/, and .beads/ produced no output, so no security-sensitive source/config/docs surfaces are staged.
br show project-readiness-kit-rel-untrack-dist-gko --json carries the release prohibition not to change license or visibility; the staged payload does not touch those files.

RusticDog Agent Mail ruling 2467: A14c clarifies governed = the payload carries regulated content/behavior OR operates directly on it (create, modify, delete); textual reference alone is not carriage.

Ruling 2467 specifically classifies rel-untrack-dist as governed because deleting the release binary archive and checksum changes the repo supply-chain surface, and directs recording security-boundaries = pass with merged evidence.

conditions.message: No governed visual-regression surface exists: the payload contains no browser, screenshot, responsive layout, canvas, visual asset, or rendered UI change.
evidence: Read backpressure/visual-regression.md; it governs screenshot, browser, responsive layout, canvas, and visual-diff work.
git diff --cached --name-status contains only two deleted dist archive/checksum paths.
git diff --cached --stat reports a binary archive deletion and one checksum-line deletion, not a visual/UI file change.

conditions.message: The only governed budget-like surface is tracked artifact size/repository bloat, and the staged payload improves it by removing the committed release archive and checksum from source control.
evidence: Read backpressure/performance-budget.md; it includes bundle-size and cost/budget thresholds as possible governed surfaces.
git diff --cached --stat reports dist/burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz as Bin 2850829 -> 0 bytes and removes the one-line .sha256 file.
git diff --cached --summary reports delete mode 100644 for both dist/burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz and dist/burpvalve_linux_amd64.tar.gz.sha256.
test -z "$(git ls-files dist)" passed, so no tracked dist artifacts remain after the staged deletion.

conditions.message: The staged action stays inside the explicit bead authorization for untracking dist artifacts and does not perform autonomous release, tag, issue, license, or visibility actions.
evidence: br show project-readiness-kit-rel-untrack-dist-gko --json explicitly scopes this bead to git rm --cached of the two dist paths and says release/tag/asset validation is later release work.
git diff --cached --name-status showed only those two paths staged.

git diff --cached --name-only grep for docs/code/.github/install/license/beads surfaces produced no output, confirming no autonomous issue closure, release upload, tag, license, or visibility change is staged.
